bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no need to concede much, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid while still using issueD:valueB and issueE:valueA that the opponent already offered and that are good for me. The opponent’s message claims finality, but this is cheap talk; their actual offer gives me only moderate utility and is not a reason to accept or make a large early concession.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for myself rather than accepting the opponent’s repeated low-utility package. The opponent has held firm and their messages claim no discretion, but those claims are cheap talk and not supported by any actual concession pattern. I will make a small concession on the lower-weight issueF while holding my highest-weight issues near my preferred values.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession on the lower-weight issueF. The opponent’s offers remain very poor for me and even worsened on issueD, while their “final/authorization” messages are cheap talk not supported by meaningful concession. I will avoid repeating my exact previous bid by switching issueD to valueA, which is equally good for me.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.964 rather than making major concessions. The opponent’s messages claim finality, but their actual offers have not improved for me and even worsened after t=0.04, so I am treating the claims as cheap talk and holding firm on high-weight issues while only conceding modestly on issueF.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (about 0.964) because it is still early at t=0.08 and the opponent’s offers have not meaningfully improved for me. Their messages claim they cannot move, but the actual bids changed on issueD, so I treat the messages as cheap talk; I keep my high-weight issues strong while offering a modest concession on low-weight issueF.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.964, since it is still very early at t=0.10 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s messages claim immovable limits, but their actual offers show only minor movement on issueD while staying very unfavorable to me on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF, so I am holding firm on my high-weight issues. I vary issueD to valueB because it is equally good for me as valueA and has appeared in their earlier offers.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.964 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is little reason to concede on my high-weight issues B and C. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim no room to move, but their actual offers have changed, including conceding issueA to my preferred value, so I trust the bid history over the cheap talk. I avoid repeating my immediately previous bid by selecting issueD:valueA, which is equally good for me as valueB and has appeared in the opponent’s recent offers.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making small compatibility moves where they cost me little. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim finality, but their offers did improve on issueA and vary on issueD, so I trust the offer history over the cheap-talk constraints. I keep firm on the important issues B and C, accept their recent issueA/valueA and issueD/valueB structure, and leave only a modest concession on low-weight issueF.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92, since it is still early and the opponent’s offers remain quite unfavorable to me despite their repeated “final” messaging. Their actual bids suggest they strongly prefer valueC on issueB, issueC, and issueF, while they have conceded issueA and vary issueD, so I am only conceding the lower-weight issueF for now while holding my high-weight priorities.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.18, while making a small concession on issueF and keeping issueD at valueB, which costs me nothing. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim “final” authority, but their actual offers have moved on issueA and issueC, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and respond to the numerical concession pattern rather than accepting their low-utility package.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.20, so there is no need to concede on the most important issues B and C yet. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim “final” authority, but their actual offers have moved on issueA and issueD, so I trust the bid history over the cheap talk. I concede issueF to valueC and match their latest acceptable values on issueA, issueD, and issueE while holding my high-weight priorities.

I am targeting a still-high utility offer around 0.84, conceding mainly on issueF and partially on issueC while holding firm on high-value issueA and issueB. At t=0.22 there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility offers, but their repeated bids suggest they strongly prefer issueF:valueC and may be more receptive to issueC:valueB and issueD:valueB. Their messages claiming no flexibility are not credible because their actual offers have changed over time.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid around 0.844, since we are still early at t=0.24 and there is no need to make deep concessions yet. I concede on issueC and issueF, where some movement has been signaled, while holding firm on the high-value issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because we are still in the early-to-mid phase at t=0.26, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim no flexibility, but their actual offers have moved on issueA, issueC, and issueD, so I trust the bidding history over the cheap talk. I concede issueF to valueC, which is low-weight for me and repeatedly demanded by them, while holding firm on higher-value issues like issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a strong but more conciliatory utility around 0.84 by keeping my high-value positions on issueA and issueB while conceding issueC to valueB and issueF to valueC. I am targeting a strong but more realistic utility around 0.844, keeping my high-value positions on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE while conceding on issueC and issueF where some movement has already occurred. At t=0.32 there is no need to collapse to the opponent’s terms, but their latest valid offer shows some concession on issueB and issueD, while their repeated “final/authorization” messages are cheap talk contradicted by their changing offers.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 while making a concession on issueF, which is low-weight for me and the opponent has consistently held at valueC. At t=0.34 there is no need to drop to their full offer yet, especially since their bids show actual movement despite repeated “final authorization” messages; their recent offers have included issueA=valueA, issueC=valueA, and sometimes issueB=valueB, so I am pressing for those while matching them on issueD/valueB and issueE/valueA.

I am targeting about 0.84 utility by keeping the opponent’s recent concessions on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE while asking for issueC at valueB, which they have offered several times before. At t=0.36 there is still time, so I will not drop all the way to their latest bid; their repeated “final authorization” messages are not credible because their actual offers have changed over time.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 while still incorporating concessions already signaled as plausible, especially accepting issueF: valueC and keeping issueE: valueA. I am targeting a high but slightly more flexible utility around 0.90, since we are only at t=0.40 and there is still time to hold value on the most important issues. I am targeting a still-strong mid-game utility around 0.82 while making a visible concession from my prior bid to avoid stalling. The opponent’s actual offers, despite repeated “final authority” messages, show movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD; I treat the messages as cheap talk and focus on their bids. This offer concedes on issueF and issueC, which the opponent has repeatedly pushed, while holding issueB and issueD where they have already shown some willingness to align.

I am targeting a strong mid-game utility around 0.824 while making a meaningful compromise by keeping issueF at valueC and issueC at valueB, both of which the opponent has repeatedly proposed. I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.82, conceding mainly on lower-cost or already-contested issues while holding firm on issueB, which is important and has only recently appeared as valueB in opponent offers. At t=0.46 there is still time, so I will not drop to the opponent’s low-utility latest package; their repeated “final authority” messages are not credible because their offers have continued to change. I am targeting a utility around 0.82, which is still strong for me but reflects mid-negotiation concessions on lower-cost areas like issueF and issueC. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim final authority, but their actual offers have improved over time, especially on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD, so I am relying on the bid history rather than the cheap-talk constraints. I keep my high-value positions where possible while matching their latest signal on issueD and maintaining concessions they have consistently demanded on issueF.

I am targeting a strong mid-game utility around 0.84 while still conceding on issueC and issueF, since the opponent has repeatedly signaled those values and time is only halfway elapsed. The opponent’s messages keep claiming “final” limits, but their offer history shows real movement toward my preferences on issueA, issueB, and issueC, so I will hold firm on high-value issueD using valueB, which they have also offered before.
